suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `db-pipeline-worker` guidance to call out that `ON CONFLICT ... DO NOTHING` is not sufficient for nullable unique keys in PostgreSQL. Workers should use `WHERE NOT EXISTS`, a `DO $$` guard, or another NULL-safe pattern when the uniqueness definition includes nullable columns.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 1 failed because `sql/inserts_microct.sql` duplicated `(NULL, range, label)` `descriptors_cat` rows on rerun, and the round 2 fix review confirmed that a NULL-safe `WHERE NOT EXISTS` pattern was required to make the inserts truly idempotent.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten `db-pipeline-worker` TDD compliance guidance or handoff checks so workers do not claim `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` when transcript evidence shows implementation edits landed before the corresponding tests were updated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The round 2 review for `fix-pixel-conversion-and-sql-idempotency` again found code edits in `sql/inserts_microct.sql` and `quantdb/extract_microct.py` before later test updates, while the worker still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`. A similar mismatch was already observed in round 1.
